title: NOT NULL & Default Values
intro: A developer's app crashes when it tries to display a user's name because the name is NULL (empty). The table allows empty names, which should have been prevented from the start. Let's fix the table definition.

teach.title: NOT NULL = Required Fields on a Form
teach.body: When you fill out a form, some fields have a red asterisk (*) meaning 'required.' You can't submit the form without filling them in. **NOT NULL** is the database version of that asterisk -- it means 'this column must have a value, you can't leave it blank.' Without NOT NULL, you get invisible empty values that cause crashes later.

teach.title: The Safety Net at the Circus
teach.body: NOT NULL and DEFAULT are like safety nets at a circus. The **NOT NULL net** catches any performer (data) that tries to jump without a harness (missing value) -- it stops the show immediately rather than letting someone fall. The **DEFAULT net** is softer -- if a performer forgets their costume, the circus gives them a standard one so the show can go on. Together, they make sure no one hits the ground.

teach.title: What is NULL?
teach.body:
**NULL** in a database means 'no value' or 'unknown.' It's not zero, not an empty string -- it's the absence of any value at all. NULL causes problems because:

1. `NULL + 5` = NULL (not 5)
2. `NULL = NULL` is false (NULL isn't equal to anything, even itself!)
3. Your code might crash trying to call `.length` on NULL

The fix? Use **NOT NULL** on columns that should always have a value.

teach.title: NOT NULL and DEFAULT
teach.code:
CREATE TABLE users (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,              -- name is required
  email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,      -- email is required and unique
  bio TEXT DEFAULT '',             -- optional, defaults to empty string
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,  -- defaults to true for new users
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()  -- auto-set to current time
);
teach.annotation: NOT NULL means the column is required. DEFAULT provides a value when one isn't specified. Use NOT NULL for essential data like name and email. Use DEFAULT for fields that have a sensible automatic value.

teach.body: NULL-related crashes are the number one reason vibecoded apps break in production. Setting up NOT NULL and DEFAULT correctly means your app will not crash when a user skips a form field -- and apps that do not crash are apps that keep their paying customers.

NOT NULL prevents empty values in columns that should always have data. DEFAULT provides automatic values when none is specified. Together, they keep your data clean and prevent NULL-related crashes in your app.

exercise.prompt: The app crashes with 'Cannot read property toUpperCase of null' when trying to display a user's name. Some users were inserted without a name, and the column allowed it. Find the column that should require a value but doesn't.
exercise.explanation: The name column is defined as just 'TEXT' with no constraint, so NULL values are allowed. When the app tries to call .toUpperCase() on a NULL name, it crashes. Adding NOT NULL ensures every user MUST have a name when they're inserted. The database will reject any INSERT that tries to leave the name empty. Option B (DEFAULT 'Unknown') would prevent NULLs but would fill your database with 'Unknown' users instead of forcing the app to collect a real name.
